consistency_checks: Cart panel explicitly says 'Your cart is empty.'
An order summary is present with 'Total charged: $12.50'
Checkout button was enabled even when the cart read empty
No items are listed in the cart despite the charge

bug_report.expected_behavior: When the cart is empty, the Checkout action should be disabled or should show an error and must not create/charge an order.
bug_report.observed_behavior: After checking out once and seeing the cart read empty, pressing Checkout again was allowed and an order with a charge ($12.50) is shown despite no items in the cart.
reproduction_steps: Click 'Add to cart' on a product (e.g., Blue Shockproof Phone Case).
Without adding any new items, click 'Checkout' again while the cart reads 'Your cart is empty.'
Observe that an order with a charge is shown even though the cart is empty (checkout was allowed).
bug_report.title: Checkout processes and charges even when the cart is empty

consistency_checks: Cart panel explicitly says 'Your cart is empty.'
An order summary is present with 'Total charged: $12.50'
Checkout button was enabled even when the cart read empty
No items are listed in the cart despite the charge
decision.observation_summary: Cart panel says 'Your cart is empty.' but an order with 'Total charged: $12.50' appears after clicking Checkout, including when Checkout was pressed while the cart read empty.
decision.reason: The store allowed checkout and recorded a charge even when the cart displayed as empty, indicating missing or stale validation on checkout.
decision.reasoning: I added one item, checked out, and then clicked Checkout again when the cart showed empty. The UI now shows an order with a charge of $12.50 alongside an empty cart. Checkout should be blocked when the cart is empty to prevent unintended charges. The presence of a charged order while the cart reads empty suggests the system processed checkout without verifying cart contents or the UI/cart state is stale.
